product_overview: CiscoAutoFlash is a Windows desktop application that automates serial- and USB-first maintenance workflows for Cisco 2960-X switches, and provides a demo/replay harness for pre-hardware validation. The current phase focuses on pre-hardware stabilization, real-hardware smoke readiness, and session artifact export.
core_goals: Provide a reliable serial/USB-first operator workflow for Cisco 2960-X maintenance (scan, stage1/2/3 flows).
Support reproducible demo and replay scenarios for pre-hardware validation without physical devices.
Produce complete session artifacts (logs, transcripts, reports, manifests, bundles) for operator traceability and offline analysis.
Keep hidden backend SSH/SCP transport available for later integration while avoiding exposing it in the current operator UI.
Stabilize the Windows onedir field bundle and avoid destabilizing the field-test baseline during pre-hardware work.

features.name: Serial/USB Target Scan and Detection
features.description: Discover connected devices over serial and USB, detect model and runtime state (rommon/running), and present actionable targets to the operator.
user_flows: CALL ciscoautoflash.core.transports.list_serial_ports() -> Returns ['COM1'] -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.scan.detect_target(port='COM9') -> Returns {'error':'no_response','port':'COM9'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.ui.notifications.show_scan_error(port='COM9',error='no_response') -> Returns {'notified':True}

features.name: Stage 1: Reboot and Config-Dialog Handling
features.description: Initiate switch reboot into the required boot mode and handle interactive configuration dialogs automatically or via operator prompts.
user_flows: CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage1.initiate_reboot(port='COM3') -> Returns {'status':'rebooting','pid':1234} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage1.await_boot_prompt(port='COM3',timeout=300) -> Returns {'prompt':'rommon'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage1.handle_config_dialog(port='COM3',auto_accept=True) -> Returns {'status':'complete'}

CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage1.initiate_reboot(port='COM3') -> Returns {'status':'rebooting'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage1.await_boot_prompt(port='COM3',timeout=60) -> Returns {'error':'timeout'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.ui.actions.offer_retry(action='stage1_reboot') -> Returns {'operator_choice':'abort'}

features.name: Stage 2: Install Flow and Timeout/Error Handling
features.description: Transfer image (USB/serial-assisted) and execute installation with progress monitoring, timeouts, and recovery paths.
user_flows: CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage2.transfer_image(method='usb',port='COM3',image_path='%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\images\\ios.bin') -> Returns {'progress':100,'status':'transferred'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage2.start_install(port='COM3') -> Returns {'status':'installing'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage2.await_install_complete(port='COM3',timeout=3600) -> Returns {'status':'success'}

CALL ciscoautoflash.core.stage2.transfer_image(method='usb',port='COM3',image_path='invalid_path') -> Returns {'error':'file_not_found'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.ui.notifications.show_error(title='Image transfer failed',details='file_not_found') -> Returns {'notified':True}

features.name: Stage 3: Verification and Reporting
features.description: Verify installed image and configuration, collect verification data, and produce a structured report of the outcome.

features.name: Session Artifact Collection and Bundle Export
features.description: Collect logs, transcripts, manifests and produce an exportable bundle for archive or support troubleshooting.
user_flows: CALL ciscoautoflash.core.artifacts.collect(session_id='sess-1234') -> Returns {'logs_path':'...\\sess-1234.log','transcript_path':'...\\sess-1234.txt'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.artifacts.create_manifest(session_id='sess-1234') -> Returns {'manifest_path':'...\\sess-1234-manifest.json'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.core.artifacts.create_bundle(session_id='sess-1234',destination='%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\bundles') -> Returns {'bundle_path':'%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\bundles\\sess-1234-bundle.zip'}

CALL ciscoautoflash.core.artifacts.collect(session_id='sess-4567') -> Returns {'error':'disk_full'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.ui.notifications.show_error(title='Artifact collection failed',details='disk_full') -> Returns {'notified':True}

features.name: Demo Mode and Replay Scenarios
features.description: Run canned replay scenarios and a demo harness to exercise GUI and workflows without hardware; store demo artifacts under the demo runtime root.
user_flows: CALL ciscoautoflash.replay.run_scenario(name='full-install',demo_root='%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\demo') -> Returns {'scenario_id':'replay-001','status':'started'} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.replay.get_status(scenario_id='replay-001') -> Returns {'stage':'stage2','progress':45} -> CALL ciscoautoflash.replay.wait_for_completion(scenario_id='replay-001') -> Returns {'status':'finished'}

features.name: Hidden Backend SSH and SCP Transport (backend-only)
features.description: Provide an SSH/SCP transport implementation for future SSH-first workflows; intentionally not exposed in the operator UI during this phase.

project.name: ciscoautoflash
project.description: CiscoAutoFlash desktop tool for Cisco 2960-X serial and USB workflows.

modules.role: Russian-first desktop dashboard and operator workflow UI
Workflow orchestration, reporting, session artifacts, transports
Demo and replay harness for pre-hardware validation
Canned scan, stage1, stage2, stage3 and full-install fixtures
Hardware smoke runbooks and readiness documentation

features: Serial-first operator workflow for Cisco 2960-X over COM and USB
Dashboard with preflight, operator status, target selection, and diagnostics tabs
Demo mode for click-smoke without hardware
Replay-backed scenarios for scan, stage1, stage2, and stage3 verification
Session artifacts including logs, transcripts, reports, manifests, and exportable bundles
Hidden backend-only SSH and SCP transport support prepared for later integration
Portable Windows field bundle already built for real-hardware smoke use

quality_state.current_goal: pre-hardware stabilization and demo GUI self-test
notes: Real-hardware scope is serial/USB only for the current phase
SSH transport exists but is not exposed in the operator UI yet
